description: QQQ default baseline with 60d plus8/minus4 drop-neutral label
keep-all binary underperformed the default QQQ baseline
60d plus10/minus5 did not solve the near-all-positive bias
60d plus6/minus3 also stayed too close to an all-positive model
40d plus8/minus4 improved validation balance but weakened test performance
60d plus12/minus8 raised the score but still behaved like a near-all-positive model
60d plus15/minus8 reduced class balance but still collapsed into an all-positive test model
60d plus12/minus10 produced a misleadingly high f1 because it predicted positive on every test row
40d plus10/minus6 remained too close to the original degenerate QQQ behavior
future-return-top-20pct improved class balance slightly but still produced a near-all-positive QQQ model
future-return-top-25pct also stayed degenerate and did not improve test balance
future-return-top-30pct kept the percentile-label idea simple but still collapsed toward all-positive predictions
ret_60 changed almost nothing and did not fix the near-all-positive bias
sma_gap_60 stayed identical to the QQQ baseline
atr_pct_20 did not change the QQQ baseline behavior
distance_to_252_high did not change the QQQ baseline behavior
close_location_20 did not change the QQQ baseline behavior
neg_weight 1.10 and 1.20 were effectively identical to baseline
neg_weight 1.30 barely reduced the positive rate and slightly hurt the score
